title: Live Microphone Sampling
description: Techniques for sampling vocals, beatboxing, and environmental sounds using the built-in microphone.

steps.title: Prepare the Environment
steps.instruction: Find a quiet space for recording. The built-in microphone picks up ambient noise easily. Turn off fans, close windows, and minimize background sounds.
steps.tip: The PO-33's mic has automatic gain, so it amplifies quiet sounds - including unwanted noise.

steps.title: Select a Sound Slot
steps.instruction: Turn the sound knob to choose where to record. Use slots 1-8 for melodic samples (vocals, tonal sounds) or 9-16 for drums (beatbox, percussion).
steps.tip: Melodic slots let you play samples at different pitches afterward.

steps.title: Position and Test
steps.instruction: Hold the PO-33 6-12 inches from your sound source. Hold record to see the input level on the display. Test your levels before committing.

substeps.instruction: Watch the level meter on display
Make your sound to check levels

steps.tip: Too close = distortion. Too far = noisy. Find the sweet spot!

steps.title: Record Your Sample
steps.instruction: While holding record, press a pad to start. Make your sound (sing, speak, beatbox). Press the pad again to stop. Release record.
steps.warning: Memory is limited! Keep samples short and punchy for best results.

steps.title: Trim the Sample
steps.instruction: If your sample has unwanted silence at the start or end, trim it. Hold record, turn sound knob to adjust start point, turn pattern knob to adjust end point.
steps.tip: Press the sound's pad while adjusting to preview the trimmed region.

steps.title: Use Your Sample
steps.instruction: Your sample is ready! Press its pad to play it. If it's a melodic sample (1-8), hold the pad and press other pads to change pitch for melodies.
steps.tip: Layer multiple vocal samples across different slots for harmonies and ad-libs.

outcomes: Understanding of gain staging with the built-in mic
Ability to trim samples for clean playback
Skills for sampling vocals, beatbox, and found sounds
